UW-3  WELDED JOINT CATEGORIES. (a) Welded joints are classified into categories based on their location in the vessel: Category A — longitudinal and spiral joints in shells and heads; Category B — circumferential joints between shells and heads; Category C — joints connecting flanges, tubesheets, and flat heads to shells; Category D — joints connecting nozzles to shells and heads. (b) The applicable joint efficiency for each category shall be determined from UW-12. Examination requirements are specified in UW-11. For design of welded joints, see UW-9. Weld sizing shall comply with UW-16 and UW-35.
